JEPA-Lens

Mapping what a model finds predictable

JEPA-Lens predicts masked patch representations instead of pixels and uses prediction error as a lens into model understanding. This dashboard now pulls the core maps, embeddings, validation figures, and training outputs into one synced static report.

32x32 CIFAR-10 0 labels during pretraining Representation-space MSE 4 controlled variants
The strongest claim is in the deltas, not a single heatmap.

Variant shifts plus the validation suite are what make the report useful. The page below is organized so you can move from intuition to evidence quickly.

Variants4Baseline plus three fine-tuned controls.
Artifact coverage17/17100% of tracked figures found in outputs/.
Best probe51.4%Baseline v2 is the top linear probe model.
Generated2026-03-26 19:45Writes both dashboard HTML files in sync.
Overview

What the project is trying to prove

JEPA-Lens treats predictability as a measurement tool. If certain regions stay hard to infer from context, those regions may carry structure the model considers important.

Core question

Which regions remain hard to predict?

Low error marks easy context completion. High error marks uncertainty, ambiguity, or complex structure.

Main finding

Prediction error is mixed, not trivial

The validation figures suggest a stable structural component plus a finer texture-sensitive component.

Why variants matter

The objective can steer complexity

Noise, edge bias, and stronger masking shift what the model treats as difficult without destroying the representation.

Pipeline

How the report maps to the workflow

Each phase creates a figure family that feeds directly into the dashboard.

01

Pretrain baseline

Learn a context-predictive representation with the standard JEPA recipe.

02

Steer the objective

Fine-tune controlled variants that alter noise, edges, and masking pressure.

03

Evaluate structure

Generate maps, embeddings, masking views, and loss curves for every run.

04

Stress-test the claim

Use signal, consistency, and causal tests to see whether the maps mean anything.

Understanding Maps

Interactive view of per-patch prediction error

Use the model rail to switch the main map, then scan the grid below for all four variants. Red patches are harder predictions, not object labels.

Baseline v2

Standard JEPA with depth 6 and multi-crop masking.

51.4% linear probe 3.1M params 60 epochs
Baseline v2 Red = harder to predict, green = easier to predict
Reference model for all comparisons.

Best probe score while keeping balanced structural and semantic sensitivity.

Representations

Embedding structure and masking behavior

The t-SNE figures show how structure organizes in representation space, while the masking view grounds the heatmaps in the actual visible context versus hidden targets.

What the encoder sees versus what it must predict.

Validation

Evidence that the maps are not just decoration

These figures test whether the predictability signal is stable, distinguishable from edge maps, and causally related to downstream semantics.

Signal nature

Not raw edges

Edge-focused training changes the maps, but the baseline signal does not collapse into a plain contour detector.

Consistency

Partly stable under transforms

Flips and crops preserve a structural component, suggesting the signal is not random patch noise.

Causality

Hard patches matter more

Ablating high-error regions should hurt probe accuracy more than removing random or easy patches.

Masking Visualization

What the encoder sees versus what it must predict.

Present

Signal Nature Test

Checks whether the maps collapse into raw edge detection.

Transform Consistency

Stability of understanding maps under flips and crops.

Present

Causal Test

Ablates high-error patches to test whether they matter more.

Present
Training

Probe summary and convergence curves

The probe scores stay close across variants, which is part of the point: the objective changes representation character more than overall viability.

Linear probe snapshot

All four models stay far above the 10 percent random baseline.

Commands

Fast path to rebuild the report

These are the commands you will actually use when refreshing results.

Train baseline

python train.py

Train variants

python train_variants.py

Evaluate a checkpoint

python evaluate.py --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline_v2.pth

Generate masking viz

python visuals.py --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline_v2.pth

Run validation suite

python signal_nature_test.py --baseline checkpoints/baseline_v2.pth --structure checkpoints/structure_focused.pth

Rebuild dashboard

python dashboard.py
